_note: Dimensional header spans for an EXTERIOR BEARING WALL supporting roof+ceiling under 30 psf ground snow (Appleton/Outagamie Co. WI, Zone 2), 24 ft building width, SPF #2. Source: IRC 2021 R602.7(1) / Wisconsin UDC Table 321.25-B. NEVER interpolate, extrapolate, or hand-add rows — every row cites a published table. LVL garage-door headers (16-18 ft) are intentionally OMITTED: they cannot be prescriptively sized and must be engineered per project, so any span beyond the largest row below correctly returns a 'confirm size with supplier' note.

rows.notes: Standard window opening. 1 jack stud per side.
2 jack studs per side per IRC.
Wide window or patio door. 2 jack studs per side.
2 jack studs per side per IRC.
Heavy load application. 1 jack stud per side.

members.disclaimer: Verify load case, actual roof span (tributary area), and local AHJ amendments. Spans apply only to single-story roof/ceiling bearing at 30 psf snow, 24 ft building width. A header supporting a second floor has drastically reduced spans. Spans beyond ~10 ft (e.g. 16-18 ft garage headers) require an engineered LVL — confirm size with supplier.
